summary: Smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin opener, soaring Lay Me Down, tight second set
blurb: The show opens with a blistering Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower that sets a high bar. Highlights include an emotive To Lay Me Down and a tight second set capped by a memorable Casey Jones encore. The band stays locked in, delivering strong transitions throughout.
review: June 28, 1992 at Deer Creek Music Center finds the Dead firing on all cylinders. From the first note of “Help On The Way” into “Slipknot” and “Franklin's Tower,” the trio launches a scorching opener that reviewers call “smoking” and “tight.” Jerry’s guitar work is dead‑on, his vocals soar on the heartfelt “To Lay Me Down,” and Phil’s bass provides those “harmonious bombs” that add depth to every groove. Bob’s rhythm is spot‑on during the sequence, while Vince’s keys keep the band clicking after Bruce’s departure. The first set flows smoothly through “Just Like Tom Thumb's Blues,” “New Speedway Boogie,” and a punchy “Smokestack Lightning.” In the second set the group builds momentum with “China Cat Sunflower” into “I Know You Rider,” then delivers a solid drum‑and‑space segment before winding down with “The Last Time,” “China Doll,” and a rousing “Around & Around.” The encore’s “Casey Jones” is described as iconic, sealing a night that many fans deem one of the best first‑set performances in years. Across all recordings the energy remains high, making this Deer Creek show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that 1992 magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive and one is mixed, giving a strong overall positive sentiment. Reviewers repeatedly praise the Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence, To Lay Me Down, and the tight second set, while only minor criticisms (vocals low in mix) appear.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with clean, crisp mix and no major dropouts; captures the strong first‑set flow and the smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin sequence perfectly.

key_highlights: Smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower opener
Soaring, emotional performance of To Lay Me Down
Tight second set capped by an iconic Casey Jones encore

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar is dead‑on throughout; vocals shine on To Lay Me Down, occasional low/rough moments in the Help segment but overall strong.
band_performance.Phil: Provides harmonious bass bombs that add depth; front‑and‑center in the mix and solid all night.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar on the Help→Slip→Franklin’s sequence is on point; vocals noted as strong on several numbers.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming with a friendly wink that lifted audience spirits; overall steady foundation.
